local_dishes.name: Plov (Uzbek Pilaf)
local_dishes.description: The national dish of Uzbekistan, plov is a fragrant rice dish cooked with carrots, onions, spices, and usually lamb or chicken
source_files: Almaty Kazakhstan/Uzbekistan and Kazhakstan- All Inclusive Guide_13032026.docx

local_dishes.name: Manti
local_dishes.description: Large steamed dumplings typically filled with minced meat and onions. Vegetarian versions with pumpkin or potatoes are also sometimes available
source_files: Almaty Kazakhstan/Uzbekistan and Kazhakstan- All Inclusive Guide_13032026.docx

local_dishes.name: Samsa
local_dishes.description: A popular street snack made from flaky baked pastry filled with meat, pumpkin, or vegetables, traditionally cooked in a clay oven known as a tandoor
source_files: Almaty Kazakhstan/Uzbekistan and Kazhakstan- All Inclusive Guide_13032026.docx

local_dishes.name: Lagman
local_dishes.description: A comforting noodle dish made with hand-pulled noodles, vegetables, and a rich broth or stir-fry sauce
source_files: Almaty Kazakhstan/Uzbekistan and Kazhakstan- All Inclusive Guide_13032026.docx

local_dishes.name: Shashlik
local_dishes.description: Skewered grilled meat kebabs, typically served with fresh onions and flatbread. One of the most widely available dishes in Central Asia
source_files: Almaty Kazakhstan/Uzbekistan and Kazhakstan- All Inclusive Guide_13032026.docx

where_to_buy: Artisan boutiques in the Old Town of Bukhara
